questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A circuit is shown below.
Question: If $A$ is an ideal ammeter, $B$ an ideal Battery of voltage $V$, and $C$ an ideal voltmeter, what will be the $\frac{\text { reading of } \mathrm{C}}{\text { reading of } \mathrm{A}}$ ?
questions.solution: Reading of $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{V}\{\mathrm{i}$ in that branch $=0\}$ Reading of $A=\frac{V}{R} \Rightarrow R$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: An ammeter and a voltmeter are connected in series to a battery with an emf of 10 V . When a certain resistance is connected in parallel with the voltmeter, the reading of the voltmeter decreases three times, whereas the reading of the ammeter increases the two times.Question: Find the voltmeter reading after the connection of the resistance.
questions.solution: Initially $\mathrm{V}_1+\mathrm{V}_2=10 \mathrm{~V} \ldots$ Finally $\frac{V_1}{3}+2 V_2=10 \mathrm{~V}$ From equation (i) \& (ii) We get $V_1=6$ volt, $V_2=4$ volt $\therefore$ Final reading $=\frac{V_1}{3}=2$ volt
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A circuit is shown below.
Question: If $B$ is an inductor of inductance $L, A$ an ideal battery of voltage $V$ and $C$ an ideal battery of voltage 2 V each connected so that the anode is facing left, what is voltage across B as soon as the circuit is connected:
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{i}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{2 \mathrm{R}}$
at $t=0$ inductor is open circuit $i=\frac{V}{2 R}$
p.d. $\mathrm{V}-\frac{\mathrm{V}}{2}=\frac{3 \mathrm{~V}}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A circuit is shown below.
Question: If $A$ is a capacitor, $B$ is an ideal ammeter and $C$ is an ideal battery of voltage $V$, what is the voltage across the capacitor?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{B}}=\mathrm{iR}_{\text {ammeter }}=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~V}_{\text {cap. }}=0$
All current will pass through ammeter
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: An ammeter and a voltmeter are connected in series to a battery with emf E $=6$ volt and negligible resistance. When a resistance $\mathrm{R}=3 \Omega$ is connected in parallel to voltmeter, reading of ammeter increases three times while that of voltmeter reduces to one third.Question: Reading of voltmeter after the connection of resistance is
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: An ammeter and a voltmeter are connected in series to a battery with emf E $=6$ volt and negligible resistance. When a resistance $\mathrm{R}=3 \Omega$ is connected in parallel to voltmeter, reading of ammeter increases three times while that of voltmeter reduces to one third.Question: Reading of ammeter before the connection of the resistance is
questions.solution:
$i_0 \times R_v=x$ and $\left(3 i_0\right)\left(\frac{3 R_v}{3+R_v}\right)=\frac{x}{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad i_0=\frac{3}{16} A$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
